find:
- **Primary endpoint:** PAM-13 Δ ≥ 5.4 points (MCID)
- **Significance level:** α = 0.05 (two-sided) for primary analysis; Bonferroni correction applied only to secondary endpoints (≤2 comparisons) with α = 0.025 for each secondary test. **Harmonisation note:** This threshold supersedes any conflicting values in THEORY.md; the single adopted value is α=0.05 (two-sided) for primary, with Bonferroni-adjusted α=0.025 for secondary endpoints. No other α thresholds (e.g., p < 0.001) are used.
- **Clarification:** The value p < 0.001 previously mentioned in THEORY.md is hereby removed as inconsistent with the primary analysis plan. All primary analyses use α = 0.05 (two-sided); secondary analyses use Bonferroni-adjusted α = 0.025.
- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

- **Power:** 0.80
- **Sample size:** N ≥ 55 per group (see Sample size calculation)
- **Stopping rule:** Adverse event rate >5% in intervention arm (any AE, assessed monthly by DSMB)
- **Interim analysis:** Planned at N=100 (50 per group) for futility only (O'Brien-Fleming boundary, α-spending = 0.005). If conditional power <20%, trial will be stopped for futility.
- **Primary analysis:** Intention-to-treat (ITT); per-protocol sensitivity analysis as secondary

## Pre-registration plan

- **OSF ID:** `osf.io/TBD` (single placeholder; to be reserved before enrollment start; reservation date: 2026-08-01; the placeholder will be replaced with the actual URL at that time). **Note:** All other OSF placeholders in core documents have been removed; only this one remains.
- **Pre-registration date:** 2026-09-01 (planned; to be confirmed upon OSF reservation)
- **Planned registration date:** 2026-09-01
- **Registered design:** Two-arm parallel RCT, superiority design
- **Primary outcome:** Between-group difference in PAM-13 change score (Δ) from baseline to 6 months
- **Analysis plan:** Independent two-sample t-test (two-sided); ITT primary, per-protocol sensitivity
- **Secondary outcomes:** Medication adherence (MMAS-8), quality of life (EQ-5D-5L), healthcare utilisation (number of hospitalisations)
- **Exploratory outcomes:** Patient activation trajectory (mixed-effects model), LLM engagement metrics (session count, message length)

find:
## Sample size calculation

- **Formula:** n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** Z_α/2 = 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-sided), Z_β = 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- **Effect size δ:** 5.4 points (PAM-13 MCID)
- **Standard deviation σ:** 10 (assumed — see justification below)
- **Calculation:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2 · 10² / 5.4² = 7.84 · 200 / 29.16 ≈ 53.8 → **n = 55 per group**
- **Total N:** 110 + 20% dropout = **132 participants**
- **Sensitivity analysis (required N per group):**
  - σ = 8: n ≈ 35
  - σ = 10: n ≈ 55 (base case)
  - σ = 12: n ≈ 78
- **Justification for σ = 10:** Ba

## Sample size calculation

- **Formula:** n = (Z_α/2 + Z_β)² · 2σ² / δ²
- **Parameters:** Z_α/2 = 1.96 (α = 0.05, two-sided), Z_β = 0.84 (power = 0.80)
- **Effect size δ:** 5.4 points (PAM-13 MCID)
- **Standard deviation σ:** 10 (assumed — see justification below)
- **Calculation:** n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · 2 · 10² / 5.4² = 7.84 · 200 / 29.16 ≈ 53.8 → **n = 55 per group**
- **Total N:** 110 + 20% dropout = **132 participants**
- **Sensitivity analysis (required N per group):**
  - σ = 8: n ≈ 35
  - σ = 10: n ≈ 55 (base case)
  - σ = 12: n ≈ 78
- **Justification for σ = 10:** Based on prior PAM-13 studies (Hibbard et al., 2005) and pilot data (TBD).

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment rate | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment, extended enrollment period |
| High dropout rate (>20%) | Medium | High | Retention strategies (reminders, incentives), per-protocol sensitivity analysis |
| LLM technical failure (downtime >24h) | Low | Medium | Redundant server, offline backup mode |
| Adverse event rate >5% | Low | High | DSMB monitoring, stopping rule |
| Data privacy breach | Low | Critical | Encryption, access control, GDPR compliance |
| PAM-13 ceiling effect | Medium | Medium | Alternative outcome measure (e.g., EQ-5D-5L) as sensitivity |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- Single-centre design limits generalisability.
- PAM-13 is self-reported; potential for social desirability bias.
- LLM intervention is a black box; interpretability limited.
- Dropout may be differential between arms.
- Sample size calculation assumes σ = 10; true variability may differ.
- Placebo effect from app usage not controlled.
- Long-term effects (>12 months) not assessed.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Insignia Health:** PAM-13 license, scoring algorithm.
- **Fraunhofer IGD:** LLM development, UI/UX design.
- **TSU (Tbilisi State University):** Patient recruitment, data collection.
- **University of Copenhagen:** Statistical analysis, trial design.
- **Additional partners:** TBD (to be confirmed before enrollment).
